title: Volume mount point will remain an empty folder in target container image
body:
Should the volume mount point appears in the target container image?

```
$ mkdir tmp && cd tmp
$ cat Containerfile
FROM registry.access.redhat.com/ubi9
RUN ls /cachi2
$ buildah build --volume /home/wshi/tmp:/cachi2:Z -t localhost/wshi-test .
STEP 1/2: FROM registry.access.redhat.com/ubi9
STEP 2/2: RUN ls /cachi2
Containerfile
COMMIT localhost/wshi-test
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob e8f6360d67c9 skipped: already exists
Copying blob 115cc0a78555 skipped: already exists
Copying blob 41f4921cad7d done   |
Copying config 7bd71c1391 done   |
Writing manifest to image destination
--> 7bd71c139108
Successfully tagged localhost/wshi-test:latest
7bd71c1391087743b2dfa7b20efa7b586f941eda23c67015c441d587c2fe3068

$ podman run -ti --rm localhost/wshi-test ls -a /cachi2
.  ..
```

comments.body: Closed this issue since it's an expected behavior https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/5604, thanks @ypu for the answer.
